articles.headline: Sean Payton called a fake punt against Buffalo Bills, players vetoed
articles.description: As the Denver Broncos made their run to the AFC Championship earlier this year, ESPN's Seth Wickersham spent all two weeks of the playoffs with coach Sean Payton.

articles.headline: Sean Payton's second chance: Inside the Denver Broncos' Super Bowl quest
articles.description: Senior writer Seth Wickersham spent two weeks with NFL football coach Sean Payton ahead of the Denver Broncos-New England Patriots AFC Championship Game.

images.name: Sean Payton [1296x1296]
images.alt: Sean Payton

images.name: 01. Sean Payton (Sidelines) [1296x864]
images.caption: This season was Sean Payton's 27th coaching in the NFL, his 18th as head coach.

images.name: 02. Sean Payton (Thermostat Illo) [999x1500]
images.caption: Payton's postseason meeting schedule forces assistant coaches to be both morning people and night owls.

images.name: 03. Sean Payton (Back-up QBs Illo) [999x1500]
images.caption: No coach in modern NFL history has lost his starting quarterback the game before the Super Bowl.

images.name: 04. Sean Payton (Bill Parcells) [1296x864]
images.caption: Bill Parcells has been a friend and mentor of Payton's for more than 20 years.

images.name: 05. Sean Payton (Energy Drinks Illo) [999x1500]
images.caption: Payton studies plays into the late hours of the night.

images.name: 06. Sean Payton (Pats Media Guide Illo) [999x1500]
images.caption: Payton maintains a tradition of dunking an opponent's media guide in water and placing it in the freezer.

images.name: 07. Sean Payton (Late night Illo) [999x1500]
images.caption: Payton can remember minutiae of old plays, even at late hours of the night.

images.name: 08. Sean Payton (500K Illo) [999x1500]
images.caption: "I found it necessary to remind everyone: We're playing for a lot of cash in this tournament," Payton told his team.

images.name: 09. Sean Payton (Fan Atmosphere) [864x1296]
images.caption: The Broncos faced the Buffalo Bills in the AFC divisional game at Empower Field at Mile High in Denver.

images.name: 10. Sean Payton (Bike Dream Illo) [999x1500]
images.caption: Payton has a recurring dream about trying to get to a game on a bike that won't move.

images.name: 11. Sean Payton (Family Photos) [864x1296]
images.caption: Above, Payton's grandfather, Robert Thomas Payton. Below, Payton's childhood home in Newtown Square, Pennsylvania.

images.name: 12. Sean Payton (Minnesota Miracle) [1296x864]
images.caption: On Jan. 14, 2018, Stefon Diggs caught a 61-yard touchdown in the final play of an NFC divisional round game to secure a Minnesota Vikings win over the New Orleans Saints.

images.name: 13. Sean Payton (Bo Nix Injury) [864x1296]
images.caption: Denver quarterback Bo Nix broke his ankle in the final minutes of the Broncos' win over the Bills.

images.name: 14. Sean Payton (Stiddy Press Conference) [1296x864]
images.caption: Jarrett Stidham takes questions from the media after being named starting quarterback for the AFC Championship Game.

images.name: 15. Sean Payton (Jerry Rice) [864x1296]
images.caption: Jerry Rice was among those Payton sought out for advice during the Broncos' playoff run.

images.name: 16. Sean Payton (Pats Pregame) [864x1296]
images.caption: Stidham became the first quarterback ever to start a conference championship game without a single attempt during that year's regular season.

images.name: 17. Sean Payton (Pats Lose) [864x1296]
images.caption: The Broncos looked superior to the Patriots at the beginning of the AFC Championship Game -- until it all unraveled.

images.name: 18. Sean Payton (Pats Post Game Illo) [999x1500]
images.caption: Payton said he missed Nix in the Broncos' loss to the Patriots.
images.alt: Illustration of Broncos post game gathering after the loss to the Patriots in the AFC Championship game

images.name: Exclusive access: Inside the Broncos' run to the AFC Championship Game
images.caption: Exclusive access: Inside the Broncos' run to the AFC Championship Game

articles.headline: Before Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce, there was RussField
articles.description: Decades before Taylor Swift met Travis Kelce, RussField was the first power couple.

images.name: LEAD: Jane Russell and Bob Waterfield [1296x729]
images.caption: Bob Waterfield and Jane Russell, known as "RussField," brought together NFL stardom and Hollywood glamour decades before sports-entertainment power couples, like Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce, became a cultural force.

images.name: 01. Jane Russell [2000x2500]
images.caption: Before she became half of RussField, Jane Russell was a Minnesota-born, Southern California-raised starlet who helped define 1940s and '50s Hollywood, including starring opposite Marilyn Monroe in "Gentlemen Prefer Blondes."

images.name: 02. Bob Waterfield [2120x2500]
images.caption: Preceding his Hall of Fame career with the Rams, Bob Waterfield married his high school sweetheart, Jane Russell. In 1945, he became the only rookie in NFL history to win MVP and a championship in the same season.

images.name: Jane Russell and Bob Waterfield - Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce [1667x2500]
images.caption: In the 1950-60s, Russell and Waterfield were the pop-culture moment (top). Swift, a 14-time Grammy winner, wed Kelce, a three-time Super Bowl winner, on July 3.

images.name: Bob Waterfield and Jane Russell [1296x1296]
images.caption: Inside Buck and Etta Waterfield’s Montana basement, towering scrapbooks preserve the fame, glamour and mythology of Buck’s parents: Hall of Fame QB Bob Waterfield and Hollywood star Jane Russell.
images.credit: Courtesy of Buck and Etta Waterfield
